name: Prema Racing
bio: F2's most successful team. Prema have won more F2 titles than any other outfit and operate the championship's most polished operation. Their drivers tend to graduate either to F1 (Leclerc, Russell, Schumacher) or to top-tier factory programmes.
notable: Multiple F2 teams' titles, alumni include Leclerc, Russell, Schumacher, Bortoleto

name: ART Grand Prix
bio: Frédéric Vasseur's old team — and arguably the foundational outfit of the modern F2 era. ART's history is essentially the history of GP2/F2 itself. Strong factory backing and an excellent driver development pipeline.
notable: Founded by current Ferrari TP Vasseur. Alumni: Hamilton, Rosberg, Leclerc

name: Hitech TGR
bio: British squad with strong technical operation. Hitech runs Ferrari and Williams juniors in 2026 — a clear bet that academy backing translates to results. Quietly built into a frontrunning team over the last three seasons.
notable: Now under TGR (Toyota Gazoo Racing) banner — Toyota's stealth return to junior single-seaters

name: DAMS Lucas Oil
bio: Storied French team with a championship pedigree across multiple junior categories. Now branded with Lucas Oil and running both an American (Crawford) and Indian (Maini) driver — DAMS deliberately positioning itself as a global development program.

name: MP Motorsport
bio: Dutch team that punched above its weight to claim the 2022 F2 teams' title. MP has a reputation for getting the most out of less-fancied drivers and rapid setup work in tight session formats.
notable: 2022 F2 Teams' Champion. 2022 Drivers' Champion (Felipe Drugovich)

name: Campos Racing
bio: Spanish team founded by Adrián Campos. Long-running F2 outfit with a knack for developing Latin talent. Campos's 2024 F2 title with Bortoleto reinforced their reputation as a serious championship contender.

name: Trident
bio: Italian squad consistently in the upper midfield. Trident's strength is engineering depth — they're rarely the fastest team in qualifying but often the smartest in race trim and strategy.
notable: F3 championship pedigree; consistent upper-midfield F2 competitor

name: Van Amersfoort Racing
bio: Family-run Dutch team with a long F3/F2 history. VAR's 2026 lineup pairs experienced hands with rookie talent — the kind of mix that can occasionally spring a surprise on a tricky weekend.

name: PHM AIX Racing
bio: Mid-grid team rebuilding after rotating multiple drivers in recent seasons. PHM is investing heavily in technical infrastructure and is a long-term project rather than an immediate title contender.
notable: Series' newest entry; rapid growth phase

name: Rodin Motorsport
bio: Formerly Carlin — bought and rebranded by Rodin Cars under Trevor Carlin's continued involvement. Carlin's F2 title pedigree is huge; Rodin retains that DNA. With Miyata aboard, an interesting season for Toyota observers.
notable: Carlin heritage; multiple F2 champions including De Vries, Norris, Russell

name: Invicta Racing
bio: British team formerly known as Virtuosi. Reigning team and drivers' champions (2024 with Doohan; 2025 contender). Excellent operation with growing factory ties — McLaren's Dunne placement is a vote of confidence.
notable: Formerly Virtuosi Racing; strong recent F2 form with top-team engineering depth
